title: The Release of Zaynab 's Husband
description: Among the captives was Abu al-'As b. al-RabF b. 'Abd al-'Uzza b. 'Abd Shams, the son-in-law of the Messenger of God, being the husband of his daughter Zaynab. Abu al-'As was one of the esteemed men of Mecca in respect of wealth, trustworthiness, and commerce. He was the son of Halah bt. Khuwaylid (sister of Muhammad's wife Khadijah) so that Khadijah was his maternal aunt. Khadijah asked the Messenger of God to marry him (to Zaynab) and he did not oppose this; this was before he had received any revelations.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 07
